docs.title: Podman Desktop
docs.text: Podman Desktop is a free, open-source GUI for managing containers and local Kubernetes clusters on macOS — a Docker Desktop alternative.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Open Terminal (⌘ + Space, type Terminal) and install Homebrew if you don't have it:
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

docs.title: Install Podman Desktop
docs.text:
1. In Terminal, run:
brew install podman-desktop

  1. Open Finder (⌃ + ⌥ + F) and go to Applications. Double-click Podman Desktop to launch it.

  2. Follow the on-screen setup wizard. When prompted, click Install to install the Podman engine.

docs.title: Install Podman CLI
docs.text:
1. In Terminal, run:
brew install podman
  1. Initialize and start the Podman machine:
podman machine init
podman machine start
  1. Verify it is running:
podman info

docs.title: Install kubectl and kind
docs.text:
kubectl is the Kubernetes CLI. kind runs clusters inside Podman containers.

  1. In Terminal, run:
brew install kubectl kind
  1. Verify both are installed:
kubectl version --client
kind version

docs.title: Install Helm
docs.text:
Helm is the Kubernetes package manager used to install charts.

  1. In Terminal, run:
brew install helm
  1. Verify:
helm version

docs.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
docs.text:
1. In Terminal, tell Kind to use Podman as its provider:
export KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_PROVIDER=podman
  1. Create a cluster named dev-cluster:
kind create cluster --name dev-cluster
  1. Verify the cluster is running:
kubectl cluster-info --context kind-dev-cluster

docs.title: Verify in Podman Desktop
docs.text:
1. Open Podman Desktop from Applications.
  2. Click the Kubernetes icon in the left sidebar.
  3. You should see kind-dev-cluster listed as the active context.

docs.title: Set kubectl Context
docs.text:
If you have multiple clusters, set the active one:

kubectl config use-context kind-dev-cluster

Check all available contexts:

kubectl config get-contexts

docs.title: Delete the Cluster
docs.text:
When you no longer need it:

kind delete cluster --name dev-cluster

docs.title: Quick Setup
docs.text:
Already read this before? Run this and you're done.

# Install everything
brew install podman-desktop podman kubectl kind helm

# Start Podman
podman machine init
podman machine start

# Create cluster
export KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_PROVIDER=podman
kind create cluster --name dev-cluster

# Verify
kubectl cluster-info --context kind-dev-cluster
